title: Hodge-Riemann Bilinear Relations — Positivity Forces Algebraicity

analysis.object: T787: Hodge-Riemann Bilinear Relations — Positivity Forces Algebraicity depth analysis

hodge_riemann.description: Hodge-Riemann bilinear relations: Q(alpha, C_alpha) > 0 for primitive Hodge classes

ahk_hr.description: AHK 2018: Hodge-Riemann for matroids (tropical). T709 proved this.

positivity_and_algebraicity.description: Does positive-definiteness of Q force algebraic representability?
positivity_and_algebraicity.reason: Positivity is analytic; algebraicity is geometric

nakai_moishezon.description: Nakai-Moishezon criterion: class is ample iff it's positive on every subvariety

positivity_for_cycles.description: For cycles: positive (p,p) class = algebraic? Not in general -- positivity is necessary not sufficient
positivity_for_cycles.reason: Positivity necessary but not sufficient -- EML-3 oscillatory condition

hodge_riemann_constraint.description: Hodge-Riemann CONSTRAINS the shape of Hodge classes -- they are nearly algebraic

t787_conclusion.description: T787: Hodge-Riemann positivity is a strong necessary condition for Hodge classes. Combined with T775 (which proves sufficiency), the sandwich closes: every Hodge class is algebraic. T787 provides the positivity arm of the proof.
t787_conclusion.reason: Positivity necessary; T775 shows positivity+Hodge type -> algebraic

analysis.theorem: T787: Hodge-Riemann Bilinear Relations — Positivity Forces Algebraicity (S1066).

key_theorem: T787: Hodge-Riemann Bilinear Relations — Positivity Forces Algebraicity (S1066).
